facts: The patient's name is Tyler.
The patient is a 56-year-old male.
The patient has difficulty swallowing.
The difficulty swallowing has lasted four to five weeks.
The patient feels like something is stuck in his throat.
The difficulty swallowing is sometimes painful.
The patient has to wash down solid food on occasion.
The patient has never fully choked.
The patient has thought about potentially choking.
The patient does not experience coughing with the swallowing difficulty.
The difficulty swallowing occurs with solids.
The patient thinks the difficulty swallowing is more likely with solids.
The patient does not notice a temperature change affecting the swallowing difficulty.
The patient denied vomiting.
The patient denied blood in his stool.
The patient denied weight loss.
The patient denied chest pain.
The patient denied shortness of breath.
The patient has diabetes.
The patient has high blood pressure.
The doctor prescribed metformin.
Metformin is 500 milligrams.
Metformin is taken twice a day.
Metformin is prescribed for diabetes.
The patient's hemoglobin A1c was 7.4.
The patient is taking his metformin medications.
The patient checks his blood sugars about 80% of the time.
The patient's blood sugar numbers are within the target range.
The patient denied nausea from metformin.
The patient denied vomiting from metformin.
The patient denied diarrhea from metformin.
The doctor prescribed norvasc.
Norvasc is prescribed for high blood pressure.
The patient's blood pressure is 120/50 in the office.
The patient checks his blood pressure at home.
The patient's blood pressure at home is sometimes higher but usually within range.
The doctor examined the patient's neck.
The patient's neck is fine.
The patient's thyroid feels normal.
The patient does not have any masses in his neck.
The patient does not have any lymph nodes.
The patient's throat looks okay.
The patient does not have any masses or swelling in his throat.
The patient does not have any carotid bruit.
The patient's lung exam is clear.
The patient's heart exam is normal.
The patient does not have any murmurs.
The patient has epigastric tenderness.
The patient does not have any masses or significant swelling in his belly.
The patient's back exam is normal.
The patient's extremity exam looks normal.
The patient's neurological exam is fine.
The doctor is concerned about a narrowing in the patient's throat.
The doctor is going to refer the patient to gastroenterology.
The doctor prescribed prilosec.
Prilosec is 20 milligrams.
Prilosec is taken once a day.
Prilosec is prescribed for reflux.
The doctor recommended the patient chew his food frequently.
The patient needs a refill for norvasc.
The doctor will send a refill for norvasc to the patient's pharmacy.
The doctor will order another hemoglobin A1c test.
The doctor will order blood work to check kidney function.
The doctor will give the patient an eye referral to ophthalmology.
The patient will continue taking metformin.
The patient will follow up with the doctor in about a month.
